questions.question: What are the three core steps of cognitive restructuring for panic?

options.text: Relax, breathe, and meditate
Avoid, escape, and recover
Catch the thought, examine the evidence, and generate a balanced alternative
Suppress the thought, distract yourself, and move on

questions.explanation: Cognitive restructuring involves three steps: (1) catch the catastrophic thought, (2) examine the evidence for and against it, and (3) generate a balanced, realistic alternative based on your actual track record. This is not thought suppression — it is evidence-based evaluation.

questions.question: Which cognitive distortion involves believing that because you feel scared, something must actually be dangerous?

options.text: Catastrophizing
Fortune telling
Emotional reasoning
Interoceptive bias

questions.explanation: Emotional reasoning is the pattern of believing that because you feel terrified, something terrible must be happening. The key challenge is: feelings are not facts. Fear can exist without actual danger — that is the definition of a false alarm.

questions.question: How does cognitive restructuring differ from 'positive thinking'?

options.text: They are essentially the same technique with different names
Cognitive restructuring uses affirmations while positive thinking uses evidence
Positive thinking tries to replace all negative thoughts with optimistic ones
Cognitive restructuring examines whether your interpretation is accurate, seeking evidence-based balance rather than forced optimism

questions.explanation: Cognitive restructuring is not about pretending everything is fine or being optimistic. It is about examining whether your automatic catastrophic interpretation is accurate, then finding a more balanced, evidence-based understanding. The goal is accuracy, not positivity.

questions.question: Why is it recommended to practice challenging catastrophic thoughts when you are calm, rather than only during panic?

options.text: Because catastrophic thoughts only occur during calm moments
Because practicing when calm makes the skill automatic and available when you need it during panic
Because cognitive restructuring is ineffective during actual panic
Because you cannot identify thoughts during a panic attack

questions.explanation: Cognitive restructuring is a skill that improves with practice. Practicing when calm — like fire drills when there is no fire — builds the neural pathways so that the balanced alternatives are more readily available during actual panic. Trying to learn a new skill in the middle of panic is much harder.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an 'evidence record' in managing panic disorder?

options.text: To document every panic attack for your doctor
To prove that you have a real medical condition
To build a collection of facts that contradicts your catastrophic beliefs over time
To track which medications work best for your symptoms

questions.explanation: An evidence record is a collection of facts that contradicts your catastrophic beliefs. For example, if you fear a racing heart means a heart attack, your evidence record would note how many times your heart raced and no heart attack occurred. Over time, this builds a powerful case against the catastrophic interpretation.
